nonAcademicRequirements: The Complementary Medicine programme does not accept applicants who have upgraded their matric results

Applicants are required to complete an assignment, visit the on campus Complementary Medicine Clinic and provide a letter of recommendation from a practitioner (Homeopath, accupuncturist or phytotherapist) as a component of the selection process
Successful graduates are required to complete the master of Health Sciences in Complementary Medicine to comply with minimum registration requirements of AHPCSA for Homeopathy or Phytotherapy
Health Sciences.rawRequirements: English: 5 (60%+), Mathematics: 4 (50%+), Physical Science: 4 (50%+), Life Science: 4 (50%+). Mathematical Literacy: Not accepted. Technical Mathematics: Not accepted. Technical Science: Not accepted. The Complementary Medicine programme does not accept applicants who have upgraded their matric results. Applicants are required to complete an assignment, visit the on campus Complementary Medicine Clinic and provide a letter of recommendation from a practitioner (Homeopath, accupuncturist or phytotherapist) as a component of the selection process. The graduate will be eligible to register with the Allied Health Professionals Council of South Africa as an acupuncture therapist. Successful graduates are required to complete the master of Health Sciences in Complementary Medicine to comply with minimum registration requirements of AHPCSA for Homeopathy or Phytotherapy.

nonAcademicRequirements: Applicants are required to undergo a selection process that includes academic results, complete a questionnaire and provide letters of recommendation/observation from two practising Doctors of Chiropractic
Potential applicants will then be invited to attend an interview
Successful applicants are required to complete the Master of Health Sciences in Chiropractic to comply with minimum registration requirements of the AHPCSA

Art, Design and Architecture.rawRequirements: Minimum APS: 25 with Maths / Tech Maths OR 26 with Mathematical Literacy. English: 5 (60%+). Mathematics: 3 (40%+). Mathematical Literacy: 4 (50%+). Technical Mathematics: 3 (40%+). Visual Art graduates find work in diverse fields of the visual arts industries. In addition to being a professional artist, graduates could work, in art education and training, art gallery and museum curating and management, art consulting, arts writing, art criticism, research and journalism, printmaking and print studio management, paper making and paper production design, community and arts project management, visual events management. Qualification Code: B8BA7Q. Minimum APS of 25 with Maths / Tech Maths OR 26 with Mathematical Literacy. The requirement for maths or maths literacy as a matric subject for international admission into the BA in Visual Art UG degree may be exempt – provided the university entrance requirements and APS score remain intact and such admission is compliant with all UG Degree requirements.

Minimum APS: 25 with Mathematics OR 26 with Mathematical Literacy. English: 5 (60%+). Mathematics: 4 (50%+). Mathematical Literacy: 5 (60%+). Technical Mathematics: Not accepted. A degree in fashion design prepares graduates for various career possibilities in the fast-paced fashion industry. Amongst other, these include being a fashion designer, a fashion entrepreneur, a pattern engineer, a fashion buyer a fashion stylist. Qualification Code: B8FD1Q. Minimum APS of 25 with Mathematics OR 26 with Mathematical Literacy.

Minimum APS: 22 with Maths OR 23 with Maths Lit. English: 5 (60%+). Mathematics: 3 (40%+). Mathematical Literacy: 4 (50%+). Technical Mathematics: Not accepted. Fashion Production Graduates can be involved in the business, manufacturing, retail and quality assurance aspects of the clothing industry. Qualification Code: D8FP1Q. Minimum APS of 22 with Maths OR 23 with Maths Lit.

Minimum APS: 25 with Mathematics OR 26 with Mathematical Literacy. English: 5 (60%+). Mathematics: 4 (50%+). Mathematical Literacy: 5 (60%+). Technical Mathematics: Not accepted. Industrial Designers improve the aesthetics, ergonomics and functionality of a broad range of products from bespoke/one-off furniture items to mass produced products such as kitchen appliances and cellphones. Qualification Code: B8ID1Q. Minimum APS of 25 with Mathematics OR 26 with Mathematical Literacy.

Minimum APS: 25 with Mathematics OR 26 with Mathematical Literacy. English: 5 (60%+). Mathematics: 4 (50%+). Mathematical Literacy: 5 (60%+). Technical Mathematics: Not accepted. Interior Designers engage with a range of interior spaces to create innovative spatial solutions with a thorough knowledge of building technology, materials and human environment needs. Qualification Code: B8BA6Q. Minimum APS of 25 with Mathematics OR 26 with Mathematical Literacy.

Minimum APS: 30. English: 5 (60%+). Mathematics: 5 (60%+). Mathematical Literacy: Not accepted. Technical Mathematics: Not accepted. Architectural professionals are involved in shaping our built environment, from the simple construction of low-cost housing projects to the most sophisticated technology employed in the erection of skyscrapers. Architectural professionals are thus employed in the design, technological resolution and management of the design and construction process of buildings. Qualification Code: B8BA3Q.

Minimum APS: 20 with Maths OR 21 with Maths Lit OR 22 with Tech Maths. English: 5 (60%+). Mathematics: 4 (50%+). Mathematical Literacy: 5 (60%+). Technical Mathematics: 5 (60%+). Jewellery Designers and Manufacturers process designs and concepts in high end commercial and contemporary commercial manufacture processes. Qualification Code: D8JD1Q. Minimum APS of 20 with Maths OR 21 with Maths Lit OR 22 with Tech Maths.

Minimum APS: 25 with Maths OR 26 with Tech Maths. English: 5 (60%+). Mathematics: 3 (40%+). Mathematical Literacy: 4 (50%+). Technical Mathematics: Not accepted. Architectural Practitioners design simple buildings and produce technological details. Qualification Code: D8AT1Q. Minimum APS of 25 with Maths OR 26 with Tech Maths.

Minimum APS: 25 with Mathematics OR 26 with Mathematical Literacy. English: 5 (60%+). Mathematics: 3 (40%+). Mathematical Literacy: 4 (50%+). Technical Mathematics: Not accepted. This degree prepares students for careers in the design, film, broadcast and advertising industries as: interaction designers, user experience designers, user interface designers, service designers, music video designers, multimedia animators, motion graphics designers, information designers, video editors, video compositors, digital photographers, digital media consultants and or creative directors. Qualification Code: B8DM3Q. Minimum APS of 25 with Mathematics OR 26 with Mathematical Literacy.

Minimum APS: 25 with Mathematics OR 26 with Mathematical Literacy. English: 5 (60%+). Mathematics: 3 (40%+). Mathematical Literacy: 4 (50%+). Technical Mathematics: Not accepted. Communication Designers are creative thinkers and problem solvers who use a range of design methods, processes and techniques to create professional communication designs, such as posters, books, magazines, corporate identity programmes, packaging and web sites, both individually and as members of teams. Qualification Code: B8CD2Q. Minimum APS of 25 with Mathematics OR 26 with Mathematical Literacy.
